response:
paragraph 1  
a condition known as oligohydramnios is characterized by low amniotic fluid levels in the womb. this condition can occur due to various factors such as placental insufficiency or fetal dehydration. the presence of this condition requires careful monitoring by a healthcare professional to ensure the health of both the mother and the baby.  

paragraph 2  
in some cases, oligohydramnios may be managed through medical interventions such as amnioinfusion or hospitalization. it is important to note that this condition is not a disease but a complication that requires appropriate care. the management of this condition is typically done under the supervision of a qualified medical team.
